perturbation.original_text: D. Any sum due VIP hereunder for which a time for payment is not otherwise specified will be due and payable within ten (10) days after the date of the postmark for an invoice therefor from VIP. If BNL fails to pay any amount due within ten (10) days from the date of the postmark for the invoice, late charges of 1-1/2% per month, or the maximum amount allowable by law, whichever is less, shall also become payable by BNL to VIP.
perturbation.changed_text: D. Any sum due VIP hereunder for which a time for payment is not otherwise specified will be due and payable within sixty (60) days after the date of an electronic notification for an invoice therefor from VIP. If BNL fails to pay any amount due within ten (10) days from the date of the postmark for the invoice, late charges of 1-1/2% per month, or the maximum amount allowable by law, whichever is less, shall also become payable by BNL to VIP.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by stating that payments are due within 60 days of electronic notification, while simultaneously stating that late charges apply if payments are not made within 10 days of the postmark of the invoice, even though the invoice is stated to be sent electronically. This creates confusion about the actual payment deadline and how it applies to any late charges.
perturbation.contradicted_text: D. Any sum due VIP hereunder for which a time for payment is not otherwise specified will be due and payable within sixty (60) days after the date of an electronic notification for an invoice therefor from VIP. If BNL fails to pay any amount due within ten (10) days from the date of the postmark for the invoice, late charges of 1-1/2% per month, or the maximum amount allowable by law, whichever is less, shall also become payable by BNL to VIP.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the payment due date to 60 days after electronic notification, but the late charges still apply if payment is not received within 10 days of the *postmark*, even though invoices are now delivered electronically. This creates a direct contradiction in timing because the reference to 'postmark' is inconsistent with the new 'electronic notification' delivery method. This falls under the definition of inconsistencies leading to in-text contradiction where different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines.

perturbation.original_text:
A. The initial term ("Initial Term") of this Agreement shall be for one year commencing on the 1st day of May, 2006 ("Effective Date"). Payments under this Agreement by BNL shall commence as provided in paragraph 5(A) hereof.
B. Unless either party gives written notice to terminate this Agreement at least six (6) months prior to the end of said Initial Term, this Agreement shall continue on a year to year basis ("Extended Term(s)") until terminated by either party by giving written notice of termination thereof to the other party at least six (6) months prior to the end of the then current Extended Term.
C. However, if, after the Initial Term, BNL terminates this Agreement during an Extended Term by giving less than six (6) months notice, in addition to any other sums due VIP under this Agreement, BNL shall pay to VIP a cancellation fee equal to the greater of (i) the Minimum Fee multiplied by the remaining number of months in the then current term, (ii) the average of the monthly billings for the six (6) month period immediately preceding the date upon which the notice of termination is received by VIP from BNL multiplied by the remaining number of months in the then current term or (iii) thirty thousand dollars ($30, 000).
perturbation.changed_text:
A. The initial term ("Initial Term") of this Agreement shall be for one year commencing on the 1st day of May, 2006 ("Effective Date"). Payments under this Agreement by BNL shall commence as provided in paragraph 5(A) hereof.
B. Unless either party gives written notice to terminate this Agreement at least six (6) months prior to the end of said Initial Term, this Agreement shall continue on a year to year basis ("Extended Term(s)") until terminated by either party by giving written notice of termination thereof to the other party at least three (3) months prior to the end of the then current Extended Term.
C. However, if, after the Initial Term, BNL terminates this Agreement during an Extended Term by giving less than six (6) months notice, in addition to any other sums due VIP under this Agreement, BNL shall pay to VIP a cancellation fee equal to the greater of (i) the Minimum Fee multiplied by the remaining number of months in the then current term, (ii) the average of the monthly billings for the six (6) month period immediately preceding the date upon which the notice of termination is received by VIP from BNL multiplied by the remaining number of months in the then current term or (iii) thirty thousand dollars ($30, 000).
perturbation.explanation: The change alters the termination notice period from six months to three months in section 15(B) while section 15(C) refers to 6 months creating conflicting terms regarding the notice period required for termination and what penalties may apply. This in-text contradiction introduces uncertainty regarding the amount of notice required for termination of the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: C. However, if, after the Initial Term, BNL terminates this Agreement during an Extended Term by giving less than six (6) months notice, in addition to any other sums due VIP under this Agreement, BNL shall pay to VIP a cancellation fee equal to the greater of (i) the Minimum Fee multiplied by the remaining number of months in the then current term, (ii) the average of the monthly billings for the six (6) month period immediately preceding the date upon which the notice of termination is received by VIP from BNL multiplied by the remaining number of months in the then current term or (iii) thirty thousand dollars ($30, 000).
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 15(B) reduces the required notice period for termination from six months to three months. Section 15(C), the contradicted text, still refers to 'less than six (6) months notice' and calculates a cancellation fee based on this timeframe. This creates a direct contradiction because the contract now specifies two different notice periods, creating uncertainty about which period applies and, consequently, how the cancellation fee should be calculated. This aligns with the 'Inconsistencies' definition provided, where different sections of the contract provide conflicting deadlines.
